title: Where to buy J-beauty and K-beauty skincare in Buenos Aires
checkedNote: Verified Jul 13, 2026 via Google Places Find Place / Text Search / Place Details (GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY_PRIVATE), plus Infobae, FashionNetwork, retailer sites/Instagram bios, and JuliJuli/Luxodia/Tina&Co public pages.

validation.source: Google Places API plus retailer sites and Argentine press
validation.criteriaNote: Physical pins require an operational or temporarily-closed Google Maps match when one exists. Online/pickup-only sellers without a public Maps listing are kept in the list but omitted from the map. Food-only Japanese/Asian grocers are included as J-beauty scarcity context, not as skincare destinations.

tldr: K-beauty is abundant in person: SKINKO (Alto Palermo flagship, Alto Avellaneda, Alcorta Shopping) plus Barrio Chino’s Tina&Co beauty floor and several WhatsApp/Instagram importers with real pickup.
J-beauty is scarce: there is no dedicated Japanese skincare walk-in store. Authentic Japanese brands are almost entirely online (MercadoLibre, iHerb) or a few SKUs at Korean importers.
Best browse-and-buy bets: SKINKO Alto Palermo or Tina&Co (Mendoza 1678) when open. For Japanese product, phone-verify JuliJuli or iFans before visiting.

recommendations.title: Fastest authentic K-beauty browse-and-buy
recommendations.body: Go to SKINKO on the 2nd floor of Alto Palermo, or Tina&Co at Mendoza 1678 in Barrio Chino when it is open. Both are true walk-in shops with recognized imported Korean brands.

recommendations.title: Specific viral Korean brands or better prices
recommendations.body: Use an established importer with pickup — Luxodia (Belgrano retiro) or JuliJuli (Villa Crespo showroom by arrangement). Confirm the exact SKU before travelling.

recommendations.title: Japanese skincare
recommendations.body: Do not expect a Japanese store. Buy online (MercadoLibre or iHerb for Biore/sunscreens) or phone JuliJuli / iFans first to confirm a Japanese item is physically in stock.

recommendations.title: Authenticity
recommendations.body: Prefer sellers that state ANMAT registration and direct-from-Korea sourcing (SKINKO, Luxodia, Skinfree, Tina&Co, JuliJuli). Treat unbranded Barrio Chino bazaar cosmetics and ultra-cheap sheet-mask racks as lower confidence.

caveats: Import-dependent stock and prices fluctuate with Argentina’s import rules and exchange rate; online listings frequently show “Sin stock.”
Tina&Co is marked CLOSED_TEMPORARILY on Google Maps and on Instagram (“CERRADO TEMPORALMENTE NOS EXPANDIMOS”) as of this check — confirm reopening before visiting.
Bajo Flores “Barrio Coreano” is a real Korean district but groceries/restaurants/textiles, not a dedicated K-beauty strip; local reporting notes insecurity near nearby villas — prefer daytime visits.
Gochiso’s public Maps listings point to a Balcarce depósito/mayorista; Instagram still promotes a San Telmo retail presence at Bolívar 892 — treat as food/bazar, not J-beauty.
keyFindings: SKINKO opened its first fixed store at Alto Palermo in December 2025; Infobae called it the first physical store of its kind in Argentina, led by Mateo Paik / Grupo SkinFree.
Barrio Chino in Belgrano remains the strongest walk-in cluster for Korean skincare, anchored by Tina&Co’s three-level market with a dedicated beauty floor.
Multiple importers (Luxodia, Skinfree/Coony, JuliJuli, K-Beauty Sur) operate online/WhatsApp with real presencial pickup around Palermo / Belgrano / Villa Crespo.
Japanese physical footprint in the city (Barrio Chino grocers, TOA Shoji, Nueva Casa Japonesa, Gochiso) is overwhelmingly food, not cosmetics.

excluded.name: The Korean Root / KSHOP Argentina / REGY Beauty / Budamarket (as map pins)
excluded.reason: Online importers mentioned in research; no reliable public Google Maps skincare storefront pin was found for a CABA walk-in location (Budamarket has a weak Maps pin with 2 reviews; REGY returned zero results).

excluded.name: Dedicated J-beauty walk-in store
excluded.reason: None found in Buenos Aires as of mid-2026 via Places search and press/retailer checks.

stores.name: SKINKO — Alto Palermo
stores.address: 2nd floor, Alto Palermo · Av. Santa Fe 3253
stores.fullAddress: Av. Sta. Fe 3253, C1091 CABA (Alto Palermo Shopping)
stores.brands: Anua, COSRX, Beauty of Joseon, Tocobo, Medicube, TIRTIR, SKIN1004, Dr. Althea and ~22 Korean makeup/skincare brands
stores.description: Flagship dedicated K-beauty walk-in store. Infobae (Dec 19, 2025) called it the first physical store of this type in Argentina; lilac lighting, Korea-imported Skin Scanner, ANMAT-registered direct-from-Korea stock. Mid-to-high price positioning.

stores.name: SKINKO — Alcorta Shopping
stores.hours: Mall hours (confirm on site; new listing)
stores.description: Newest SKINKO mall store (~90 m²). Infobae covered the June 19, 2026 inauguration. Google Maps listing is new (1 review at check) but pin matches Alcorta Shopping.

stores.name: SKINKO — Alto Avellaneda
stores.fullAddress: Gral. Güemes 897, B1873BOC Avellaneda, Buenos Aires
stores.description: SKINKO mall branch outside CABA proper (Avellaneda). Instagram bio lists Alto Palermo | Alto Avellaneda | Alcorta Shopping.

stores.name: Tina&Co Market
stores.hours: Usually daily 9:00–20:00 — currently closed temporarily for expansion
stores.brands: COSRX, Medicube, SKIN1004, Anua, Mary&May, Biodance, VT, Purito, Arencia, Lador + Korean haircare/beauty-tech
stores.description: Three-level Barrio Chino market (Market / Beauty / Home) with a large dedicated K-beauty floor — historically the highest-confidence browseable Korean stock outside SKINKO. Google Maps + Instagram both say temporarily closed for expansion as of this check; confirm before visiting. WhatsApp +54 9 11 5906-7323.

stores.name: Grupo Skinfree / Coony
stores.brands: Coony, Konjac, Bek, Shisso + SKIN1004, Anua, Celimax, Mixsoon distribution
stores.description: Korean-Argentine importer group (founded 2015) behind SKINKO. Official importer positioning with ANMAT-compliant lines. Maps pin is the Grupo Skinfree Co. address — confirm pickup/visit rules before arriving.

stores.name: iFans Beauty
stores.brands: Mostly Korean (COSRX, Some By Mi, Laneige) plus some Biore (Japanese)
stores.description: Small physical Belgrano location. Useful if you need to phone-verify a Japanese Biore SKU in person; narrow weekday morning hours.

stores.name: K-Beauty Sur Argentina
stores.brands: Korean skincare importer catalog (confirm current SKUs)
stores.description: Google Places-verified K-beauty importer with a public CABA address. Small review volume; arrange/confirm before visiting (apartment/office-style pickup).

stores.name: Casa China
stores.brands: Primarily food; limited Korean sheet masks (Foodaholic, MJCare)
stores.description: Asian supermarket that stocks some Korean sheet masks — cheaper than Tina&Co per local bloggers, but not a full-routine K-beauty destination. Lower-confidence authenticity tier for cosmetics.

stores.name: JuliJuli Beauty K-Shop
stores.address: Private showroom · Corrientes & Julián Álvarez (exact address by WhatsApp)
stores.fullAddress: Villa Crespo, CABA · exact address shared privately
stores.brands: Beauty of Joseon, Anua, COSRX, SKIN1004, Round Lab, Isntree, Torriden, Medicube, Tocobo, Purito + some Japanese (Biore, Hada Labo, Rohto Skin Aqua, Kose)
stores.description: Importer since 2016 with a private Villa Crespo showroom (not open walk-in). Broad Korean catalog plus a few Japanese items — arrange visit in advance. No public Google Maps business pin.

stores.name: Luxodia
stores.fullAddress: Online · Belgrano retiro after reservation · luxodia.com
stores.brands: Anua, Beauty of Joseon, Biodance, Celimax, COSRX, Dr. Althea, Medicube, Mixsoon, SKIN1004, Tocobo, VT, and more
stores.description: Online-first K-beauty importer advertising ANMAT-approved originals from Korea with presencial Belgrano retiro. No public Maps storefront pin — reserve pickup via the site.

stores.name: Supermercado Ichiban
stores.brands: Japanese/Asian food only — no Japanese skincare on shelves
stores.description: Included as J-beauty scarcity evidence: Japanese physical retail in Barrio Chino is food (soy, nori, noodles, fish), not cosmetics.

stores.name: Nueva Casa Japonesa (Kometo)
stores.brands: Japanese food importer retail — food only
stores.description: Major Japanese-food importer storefront. Food only — no J-beauty skincare line.

stores.name: TOA Shoji
stores.description: Large Japanese food importer with depot visits for retail food purchases. Own site: food products from Japan — no cosmetics.

stores.name: Gochiso Productos Japoneses
stores.fullAddress: San Telmo retail historically Bolívar 892; Google Maps listings at Balcarce 1322, C1153 CABA
stores.brands: Japanese food, snacks, bazar, kawaii gifts — no J-beauty skincare line
stores.description: Japanese food/bazar retailer. Instagram still mentions Bolívar 892 San Telmo; public Google Maps pins are the Balcarce depósito/mayorista. Not a skincare destination.
